title: Learned Bandwidth Throttling for Mobile Users
summary: Adapt upload pacing to recipient network quality on phones, LTE links, and unstable Wi-Fi without manual tuning.
methods: Start with EWMA-based RTT/loss estimation, then layer a small contextual bandit or TinyML policy over QUIC pacing and chunk scheduling.
metrics: Completion time, goodput, tail latency, fairness, retransmission rate, and energy per gigabyte.
wedge: Ship heuristic pacing first and treat the model as a learnable replacement once telemetry is available.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof of File Delivery
summary: Prove that the recipient received the file matching a published Merkle root without exposing the file contents.
methods: Use staged evidence: signed segment receipts, a final authenticated delivery receipt, and optional Halo2 or Plonky-style succinct proof wrapping the receipt chain.
metrics: Proof size, prover time, verifier time, and additional transfer overhead per file.
wedge: Do not block MVP on ZK; implement signed receipts first and add succinct proof generation behind a feature flag.

title: Per-File Differential Privacy for Exposed Metadata
summary: Protect outward-facing metadata such as public filenames, approximate sizes, and timestamps without corrupting canonical internal file records.
methods: Apply alias labels, size bucketing, timestamp coarsening, and a bounded privacy-budget ledger for metadata surfaces that leave the local host.
metrics: Privacy loss budget, inference error, usability cost, and storage overhead introduced by padding.
wedge: Make the feature opt-in and separate user-visible aliases from internal immutable manifests.

title: Verifiable Conflict-Free Delivery
summary: Allow concurrent updates or multi-uploader handoff while preserving deterministic final state and auditability.
methods: Combine a Merkle DAG, CRDT-style causal metadata, and merge receipts signed by participating devices.
metrics: Convergence rate, merge determinism, proof size, and user-visible conflict rate.
wedge: Begin with append-only immutable files and later extend to multi-writer manifests.

title: Energy-Aware Upload Scheduling
summary: Delay or reshape large uploads when the host is on battery or under thermal pressure while still honoring urgent transfers.
methods: Use OS battery state, thermal hints, and a priority queue with deadline-aware admission control.
metrics: Joules per gigabyte, completion-rate under deadlines, battery drain, and user override frequency.
wedge: Implement rule-based scheduling first; later learn better thresholds from local history.

title: UPnP / NAT-PMP Automatic Port Forwarding with Proof
summary: Automatically request router mappings so a home PC can expose a share link while it is online, then record evidence of successful reachability.
methods: Probe IGD or NAT-PMP, create mappings, run an external echo test, and sign a reachability descriptor bound to the current share session.
metrics: Mapping success rate, time to public reachability, failure causes, and relay fallback rate.
wedge: Use signed external probes in the first release; any ZK representation of reachability is strictly a later optimization.

title: Privacy-Preserving Public Relay
summary: Provide an optional relay for cases where home routers or ISPs block direct access, without exposing file contents to the relay operator.
methods: Terminate only encrypted transport metadata, use blinded session identifiers, and forward ciphertext over QUIC streams.
metrics: Relay overhead, latency inflation, metadata surface, and relay utilization ratio.
wedge: Keep relay stateless where possible and separate accounting from payload forwarding.

title: WebRTC / ICE Hole-Punching with Verifiable Direct Connection
summary: Attempt direct peer connectivity before resorting to a relay, and keep evidence of which path was selected.
methods: Use ICE candidate gathering with STUN and TURN assistance, then sign the selected candidate pair and bind it to the share session.
metrics: Direct-path success rate across NAT types, setup latency, and relay avoidance percentage.
wedge: Treat ICE as a secondary transport path after ordinary public-port reachability.

title: Dynamic DNS with Cryptographic Binding
summary: Bind a short name to the currently reachable host address while preventing stale or unauthorized rebinding.
methods: Publish signed address updates linked to the host identity key and optionally mirrored in an append-only public binding log.
metrics: Update latency, stale-resolution rate, key continuity, and rebinding failure rate.
wedge: Start with a simple rendezvous name and signed JSON descriptor before integrating external DNS APIs.

title: Verifiable Link Expiration
summary: Ensure shared links expire on time and leave auditable evidence that the capability is no longer valid.
methods: Use capability tokens with explicit expiry epochs, revocation manifests, and signed expiry events tied into the audit tree.
metrics: Expiry precision, post-expiry access rejection rate, and revocation propagation delay.
wedge: Start with deterministic token expiry and then add deletion evidence for associated storage objects.

title: Merkle-Based Verifiable Audit Logs
summary: Record all upload, link-creation, download, and expiry events in a tamper-evident structure that yields compact inclusion proofs.
methods: Use an append-only Merkle tree or log-backed DAG with signed checkpoints and exportable witness paths.
metrics: Append throughput, proof size, checkpoint cost, and audit export latency.
wedge: Make every important state transition append to the log before introducing advanced proof compression.

title: Post-Quantum Secure Link Protection
summary: Protect long-lived links and exported evidence against future quantum attacks by using hybrid classical and post-quantum primitives.
methods: Adopt crypto agility with X25519 plus ML-KEM for key establishment and Ed25519 plus ML-DSA for signatures, while preserving backward compatibility.
metrics: Handshake size, CPU cost, verification latency, and compatibility across devices.
wedge: Ship with hybrid mode before considering PQ-only modes.

title: Verifiable Deletion with Proof of Erasure
summary: Provide practical deletion evidence for consumer hardware by combining key destruction, garbage collection evidence, and audit commitments.
methods: Use envelope encryption, per-object key shredding, storage compaction reports, and attested deletion events rather than impossible claims of perfect physical wipe.
metrics: Time to logical erasure, residual accessibility probability, and user confidence signals.
wedge: Document clearly that the first implementation proves crypto-shredding and system state transition, not microscopic physical media erasure.

title: Cross-Device Verifiable Sync
summary: Let multiple devices maintain a consistent SafeDrop namespace with deterministic conflict handling and signed state continuity.
methods: Use CRDT-inspired state exchange, signed operation logs, and checkpoint hashes bound to device identities.
metrics: Replication lag, divergence incidents, merge determinism, and number of conflict-free updates.
wedge: Sync manifests first; defer full folder mirroring until state propagation is stable.

title: Regulatory-Compliant Export
summary: Create export bundles that combine files, access histories, and policy declarations in a way that is readable to legal or compliance reviewers.
methods: Emit structured JSON and human-readable PDF or DOCX summaries containing hashes, timestamps, consent notes, and provenance pointers.
metrics: Export completeness, validator success rate, and manual review time.
wedge: Support one structured evidence bundle format before adding jurisdiction-specific variants.

title: Oblivious Access Modes
summary: Offer a high-sensitivity mode that reduces leakage from storage-access patterns, particularly for repeated reads of valuable shares.
methods: Adapt Path ORAM-style techniques and block reshuffling to flash-friendly storage layouts with bounded overhead.
metrics: Bandwidth overhead, latency inflation, stash size, and access-pattern indistinguishability.
wedge: Keep this strictly optional and reserve it for small, highly sensitive datasets.

title: Verifiable Multi-Party File Sharing
summary: Support one-to-many delivery where each recipient yields a distinct receipt while the sender still obtains a compact view of campaign completion.
methods: Issue recipient-scoped capability tokens, aggregate receipts, and optionally use threshold acknowledgments for group completion.
metrics: Receipt aggregation efficiency, recipient isolation, and completion visibility.
wedge: Implement independent recipient receipts before any threshold cryptography.

title: Learned False-Positive Filtering for Access Logs
summary: Reduce operator fatigue by suppressing repetitive benign alerts while keeping potentially meaningful anomalies visible.
methods: Use on-device causal or probabilistic features over session history, link age, device fingerprints, and failure patterns.
metrics: Alert precision, recall on malicious simulations, and reviewer time saved.
wedge: Keep conservative defaults and make the filter explain why an alert was suppressed.

title: Energy-Proportional Cryptographic Profiles
summary: Adjust proof batching, chunk sizes, and hybrid-crypto policy selections to preserve battery life without dropping below a secure baseline.
methods: Use an explicit policy floor, device power telemetry, and a profile selector that chooses among approved crypto and proof pipelines.
metrics: Energy cost, proof latency, battery impact, and percentage of sessions using each profile.
wedge: Never weaken authenticated encryption below the minimum supported policy; prefer deferral and batching over weaker protection.

title: Wasm-Based Custom Sharing Rules
summary: Allow users to define advanced access policies without embedding unsafe scripting runtimes into the single-binary server.
methods: Run deterministic WebAssembly policy modules in a sandbox with capability-scoped host calls and execution tracing.
metrics: Policy execution latency, memory limits, determinism, and rule-debugging effort.
wedge: Start with a fixed rule DSL and promote it to Wasm once the host interface is stable.

title: Post-Quantum Key Rotation
summary: Rotate long-lived keys on a schedule that preserves continuity for existing links and evidence bundles.
methods: Maintain signed key-transition chains, hybrid rekey ceremonies, and rotation checkpoints inside the audit log.
metrics: Rekey duration, failure recovery rate, and continuity verification cost.
wedge: Implement explicit versioned keys before any automatic rekey daemon.

title: Verifiable Relay with Honesty Proofs
summary: Move beyond encrypted forwarding and ask the relay to produce evidence that it forwarded the right transcript without tampering or selective omission.
methods: Explore transcript commitments, chunk-level signed counters, and eventually succinct proof systems over relay forwarding traces.
metrics: Proof size, forwarding slowdown, and robustness against omission or replay.
wedge: Treat this as a frontier track layered on top of the simpler encrypted relay from R07.

title: Cross-Platform Binary Optimization
summary: Keep the one-binary promise while tuning performance for x86_64, ARM64, and lightweight edge devices.
methods: Use architecture-specific code paths, feature detection, and benchmark-guided build profiles.
metrics: Binary size, startup time, memory footprint, and throughput across platforms.
wedge: Prioritize x86_64 and ARM64 first; treat other targets as opportunistic.

title: On-Device Vector Search over Shared Files
summary: Enable semantic retrieval over local shared content without shipping raw documents to an external indexing service.
methods: Use local embeddings, a compact approximate nearest-neighbor index, and privacy-preserving query logging or noise injection.
metrics: Search latency, embedding storage cost, relevance, and metadata leakage risk.
wedge: Limit the first release to user-opted local search and avoid remote model dependencies.

title: Long-Term Evolutionary Sharing Policy Improvement
summary: Explore whether SafeDrop can locally evolve better default policies over time while keeping the resulting policy history auditable.
methods: Use bounded search over expiry windows, throttling, relay preferences, and notification policies with explainable fitness functions.
metrics: Policy quality improvement, user override rate, and stability of learned policies.
wedge: Treat this as an optional research playground, never as an autonomous unreviewed control path for security-critical settings.

title: Proof-Carrying Deduplication with Content-Defined Chunking
summary: Deduplicate repeated content without breaking the ability to prove exactly which bytes were shared and delivered.
methods: Use content-defined chunking, chunk manifests, and witness paths that rebind deduplicated chunks to each file root.
metrics: Dedup ratio, chunking overhead, proof reconstruction cost, and storage savings.
wedge: Implement fixed-size chunking first and only later switch to content-defined chunking for better dedup effectiveness.

title: Forward-Secure Ephemeral Capability Links
summary: Reduce damage from leaked links by using one-time or rolling link secrets that cannot expose earlier or later sessions.
methods: Derive capability URLs from ratcheting secrets and bind them to explicit epochs and recipient scopes.
metrics: Link-rotation cost, compromise window, and access failure rate under rotation.
wedge: Ship expiring single-use links before more advanced ratcheting.

title: Resumable Verifiable Transfer Receipts
summary: Support interrupted uploads or downloads without sacrificing strong delivery evidence.
methods: Issue segment-level receipts, checkpoint byte ranges, and aggregate the final receipt over the full manifest once all segments arrive.
metrics: Resume success rate, additional metadata per segment, and user-visible recovery speed.
wedge: Make segmented transfer a foundational primitive before adding ZK wrapping.

title: Local-First Secret Splitting and Recovery
summary: Provide disaster recovery without handing master recovery secrets to a centralized operator.
methods: Use threshold secret splitting across trusted devices, printable recovery codes, or offline hardware tokens.
metrics: Recovery success, setup burden, and probability of lockout under realistic failure scenarios.
wedge: Start with two-of-three recovery between desktop, phone, and printed emergency code.

title: Namespace Isolation and Accountable Delegation
summary: Allow the owner to delegate limited management rights or team spaces without creating a heavyweight multi-user admin system.
methods: Define namespace-scoped capabilities, delegation chains, and revocation points logged in the audit tree.
metrics: Delegation clarity, revocation speed, and accidental privilege escalation rate.
wedge: Keep delegation explicit and shallow before supporting nested teams.

title: Private Recipient Authentication Tokens
summary: Authenticate recipients to a link without forcing account creation or exposing more identity metadata than necessary.
methods: Use one-time secrets, blinded tokens, or anonymous capability upgrades that bind only to the minimum required claims.
metrics: Metadata minimization, authentication failure rate, and friction compared with ordinary links.
wedge: Single-use shared secrets are sufficient for the first iteration; anonymity-preserving token systems come later.

title: Geo- and Latency-Aware Relay Selection Under Privacy Constraints
summary: Choose a relay path that is fast enough without overexposing user geography or network identity.
methods: Score relays using coarse regions, latency buckets, and privacy-weighted routing policies.
metrics: Median latency, relay churn, and amount of geographic leakage.
wedge: Use coarse regional hints instead of exact location data.

title: Multi-Relay Route Diversity with Availability Proofs
summary: Increase link resilience by using multiple relays or relay failover paths while recording which route preserved availability.
methods: Use parallel standby circuits, route health checks, and signed continuity records for path switches.
metrics: Downtime, switchover time, and incremental relay cost.
wedge: Implement cold-standby failover first; only later explore simultaneous multipath forwarding.

title: Transport Agility for Restricted Networks
summary: Maintain connectivity across enterprise, campus, or hostile middleboxes by switching among approved transport encodings.
methods: Offer QUIC first, then HTTPS or WebSocket tunneling and conservative keep-alive strategies when networks are restrictive.
metrics: Connection success under constrained networks, handshake delay, and detection of blocked paths.
wedge: Keep the transport matrix small and auditable rather than turning SafeDrop into a general tunneling tool.

title: Reachability Forecasting for Sleeping or Intermittent Hosts
summary: Predict when a home PC is likely to be online and expose that expectation to the sender and recipient.
methods: Use local uptime history, power state transitions, and coarse recurrence patterns to estimate future availability windows.
metrics: Forecast accuracy, missed-online events, and user trust in availability indicators.
wedge: Rule-based forecasting based on recent uptime windows is enough to start.

title: Secure Wake Assist with User-Mediated Confirmation
summary: When the host is sleeping rather than fully offline, offer a safe way to wake it with explicit user control.
methods: Use authenticated local wake support, trusted companion devices, and user-confirmed wake requests instead of silent remote wake behavior.
metrics: Wake success rate, false wakes, and time-to-available after wake.
wedge: Treat wake assist as opt-in and require explicit enrollment of the waking device.

title: Delay-Tolerant Drop Queues with Eventual Verifiable Delivery
summary: Allow recipients to request a file while the host is offline and receive it later once the host returns online.
methods: Use queued delivery intents, signed request records, and eventual completion receipts anchored in the audit log.
metrics: Queue retention reliability, delivery completion after reconnection, and user satisfaction.
wedge: Start with offline request capture and notification; actual queued payload relay can come later.

title: Cross-NAT Device Assist for Pairwise Access
summary: Use a secondary personal device such as a phone or Raspberry Pi as a lightweight introducer when direct home-host reachability is poor.
methods: Bind auxiliary devices to the owner identity and let them act as rendezvous mailboxes or assistive introducers.
metrics: Setup complexity, improved connection rate, and auxiliary-device resource use.
wedge: Require explicit enrollment and keep the assist device unable to decrypt payloads.

title: Proof-Carrying Dynamic DNS Failover and Key Continuity
summary: When address changes or a relay takeover occurs, prove that the new endpoint remains the same logical SafeDrop host.
methods: Use signed continuity descriptors, rotating reachability manifests, and audit-linked binding changes.
metrics: Failover time, stale link rate, and continuity verification success.
wedge: Treat key continuity as the primary invariant, not stable IP addresses.

title: Autonomous Link Survival Analytics and Self-Healing Network Policy
summary: Let SafeDrop learn which network strategies tend to work best for a given host and adapt accordingly.
methods: Observe repeated path failures, relay performance, router behavior, and availability history to reorder future reachability attempts.
metrics: Reduction in failed link setups, mean time to successful path selection, and number of user interventions avoided.
wedge: Keep the policy engine explainable and reversible; learning must never obscure why a path was chosen.

title: Transparent Remote Attestation of Single-Binary Builds
summary: Give users confidence that the downloaded executable corresponds to a known build process and expected source state.
methods: Use signed build manifests, runtime self-reporting of version measurements, and optional external attestation checks.
metrics: Attestation verification cost, release reproducibility, and user trust indicators.
wedge: Begin with signed manifests and hash publication before pursuing hardware-backed runtime attestation.

title: Reproducible Builds and Proof-Carrying Release Artifacts
summary: Make release binaries reproducible so the community can independently verify that a published release matches the tagged source.
methods: Pin toolchains, capture build manifests, compare deterministic artifacts, and publish verification instructions with every release.
metrics: Bit-for-bit reproducibility rate, build variance, and time to verify a release artifact.
wedge: Integrate reproducible packaging into CI before the first public release.

title: Side-Channel-Aware Storage Layout for Home Devices
summary: Reduce leakage from filesystem metadata, cache timing, and repeated block placement on commodity systems.
methods: Use padded writes, metadata normalization, and careful background maintenance strategies.
metrics: Residual side-channel leakage, write amplification, and latency overhead.
wedge: Document the leakage surfaces early and prioritize the ones that matter most in real home-hosted deployments.

title: Memory-Safe Zero-Copy Encrypted I/O Pipeline
summary: Keep the server efficient enough for laptops and small boards while preserving Rust’s memory-safety advantages.
methods: Use streaming chunk pipelines, bounded buffers, and zero-copy handoff between storage, hashing, and transport stages where feasible.
metrics: Peak RSS, CPU load, throughput, and copy count per gigabyte transferred.
wedge: Instrument the data path before micro-optimizing it.

title: PQ Migration Policy Synthesis and Crypto Agility Proofs
summary: Model how SafeDrop should migrate from classical to hybrid and eventually post-quantum-first modes without breaking stored evidence or old links.
methods: Represent crypto policy as signed versioned state and prove each migration path preserves verification semantics.
metrics: Migration downtime, verification compatibility, and policy rollback safety.
wedge: Solve version negotiation and evidence-format compatibility before optimizing crypto selection.

title: On-Device AI for Link Expiry, Abuse Detection, and Policy Suggestions
summary: Use local models to suggest practical defaults such as shorter expiry for sensitive shares or relay fallback for troublesome recipients.
methods: Train small local models over past transfer behavior and expose only advisory suggestions to the operator.
metrics: User acceptance rate, reduced manual tuning, and false-alarm rate on abuse suggestions.
wedge: All AI outputs remain advisory until the operator approves them.

title: Human-Understandable Proof Summaries and Explainable Cryptographic UX
summary: Translate hashes, receipts, and proofs into plain-language evidence that non-cryptographers can still use during disputes or audits.
methods: Map protocol state into concise evidence cards, timelines, and machine-checked natural-language summaries.
metrics: Comprehension in user testing, time to find evidence, and reduction in operator confusion.
wedge: Design the explanation layer in parallel with the audit model rather than as an afterthought.

title: Carbon- and Energy-Aware Sharing Optimization
summary: Schedule large transfers to align with battery health, network cost, and sustainability goals where the user cares about them.
methods: Combine local power state, optional carbon-intensity estimates, and deadline-aware transfer scheduling.
metrics: Energy saved, completion delay, and user satisfaction with green scheduling choices.
wedge: Treat carbon data as optional and keep scheduling overrides simple.

title: Federated Reliability Telemetry Without Raw Data Leakage
summary: Improve heuristics across many SafeDrop deployments without centralizing raw transfer logs or sensitive metadata.
methods: Use local summary extraction, secure aggregation, and model updates that never upload raw evidence.
metrics: Model improvement per round, communication overhead, and privacy guarantees for participants.
wedge: Do not collect any telemetry by default; federation is opt-in research infrastructure.

title: Formal Specification and Model Checking of Share and Relay Protocols
summary: Model the protocol states rigorously so security and lifecycle invariants can be checked before code complexity grows.
methods: Write TLA+ or PlusCal specifications for share creation, path selection, receipt finalization, expiry, and relay failover.
metrics: Invariant coverage, counterexamples found, and protocol bugs removed before implementation.
wedge: Model the smallest share lifecycle first and treat the specification as a release artifact.
